spec_match.rationale: Listens for `beforeinput` and `paste` on a contenteditable, surfaces `inputType` and `dataTransfer.items`, and renders inserted images as thumbnails — the exact spec contract.

interactive.rationale: Live event monitor, simulate-insertion button, thumbnail strip, file-metadata readout. Real listener wired to a real DOM editor.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises event flow, dataTransfer object inspection, and image previews — all delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers the happy path and synthetic insertion, but does not surface what happens when `dataTransfer.items` contains a mix of image and text items (Android IMEs sometimes commit both), nor a `paste` vs `IME insert` differentiator beyond inputType.

references.rationale: The chromestatus link in see-also points at https://chromestatus.com/feature/6047872673677312 — that is NOT this feature. The correct ID for `Android IME media insertion` is 5484282563919872 (per the feature index and the chromestatus catalogue). This both breaks the self-heal script and misdirects readers.

design_system.rationale: Variables throughout, but the page sprinkles unicode glyphs (`✓`, `✕`, `⚠`, `→`) into the event log; convention is inline SVG.

openQuestions.title: Fix the chromestatus reference ID
openQuestions.detail: The see-also link currently reads `chromestatus.com/feature/6047872673677312` — wrong feature. Correct ID is 5484282563919872. This needs to be updated immediately because the slug-recovery script trusts the ID it finds in each page.

openQuestions.title: Replace unicode glyphs with inline SVG
openQuestions.detail: The event monitor uses `✓` / `✕` / `⚠` characters to mark accept/reject/warning. Project rule is inline SVG with `currentColor`. Replace each.

openQuestions.title: Demo mixed-content dataTransfer
openQuestions.detail: Some Android IMEs commit a sticker AND a text fallback together. Show what `dataTransfer.items` looks like when length > 1 and types differ.

openQuestions.title: Distinguish IME insertion from clipboard paste
openQuestions.detail: Both fire `beforeinput` with similar inputTypes. Add a column that explicitly labels each event by `event.isComposing`, `event.inputType`, and `event.dataTransfer.types` so readers can tell them apart.

summary: Useful event monitor with real image preview, but undermined by a chromestatus reference link pointing at the wrong feature ID — that needs fixing first. Then swap the glyph icons for SVG.
